introSubject: Partner Paperwork — The Part Where We Make It Official
introBody: [warm] Alright. So you went and did the first job. Which means it is time for the paperwork I have been putting off for eleven years.

Space Cowboys, Inc. You and me, fifty-fifty, Mars as the home base. Cheap dust, decent gravity, nobody breathing down our necks. Marta thinks you're ready. I told her she was high. She said she only sells to handsome men with stable orbits, so.
Here is what we need to do before we drop the pin on the rust planet:
1) One EVA contract — proves you can suit up and clock out clean.
2) Two asteroid jobs off my board — lander log reads Mercer twice, which starts to look like a pattern.
3) Cryo Insulation Lvl 1 fitted at any engineering bay — Mars nights bite and I am not explaining frostbite to your next of kin.
4) Burn for Mars and dock. I will be standing there looking insufferably pleased with myself.
5) Pick up one orbital from our new kiosk and run it. First job as a real Cowboys office. The ribbon-cutting.
Accept and I will walk you through it one step at a time.

steps.subject: One EVA — Suit Up, Clock Out Clean
flavor: Hey, you got Jay.
First one is the suit job. Grab any EVA contract off the board, get out there, and finish it without losing a glove or a tether.
Easy money for a guy with hands. You are a guy with hands. I have it on good authority.
Close it out clean and we tick the first box. Go.
— Jay

steps.subject: Two Rocks Off My Board — The Mercer Stamp
flavor: Hey, you got Jay.
[laughs] I am the dispatcher and the pilot and the guy who is buying you a beer when this is done. Welcome to early-stage equity.
Take two asteroid contracts off my list specifically — not anyone else's, mine. Boots on the rock both times. Lander out, cargo bay open with F, bring something home.
Two of mine and we start reading like a real outfit on paper. The ledger does not lie, even when everything else does.
— Jay

steps.subject: Cryo Insulation Lvl 1 — Mars Nights Bite
flavor: Hey, you got Jay.
Mars is not Venus, but the long nights have a way of finding every gap in a hull. Get Cryo Insulation Lvl 1 at any spaceport engineering bay before you head out.
First time cabin frost crawls up the inside of the canopy you will wish you had paid the credits. I paid the credits. Took me two cold nights to learn that. You get to learn it for free.
— Jay

steps.subject: Come To Mars — I Have Two Coffees
flavor: Hey, you got Jay.
Burn for Mars. Aim prograde off whatever rock you are near, let gravity do the coasting, and dock at the spaceport when the nav says you are in.

I will be the guy holding two coffees and a key card. There is a Marines bunker on Phobos — Cmdte. Sampaio runs it. He will find you when he wants you. For now: park, drink the coffee, and welcome to HQ.
— Jay

steps.subject: First Job From The Cowboys Desk
flavor: Hey, you got Jay.
Welcome aboard, partner. Now make it official. Walk into the Mars spaceport, pull up the mission board, grab the planetary shuttle job on offer, and run the orbital pickup.
The slip says Space Cowboys, Inc. on the giver line. That is the ribbon-cutting. Do not overthink it.
[sighs] Eleven years I have been out here. Would have done this a lot sooner if I had known the partner was just sitting on the Moon.
— Jay

completionSubject: We Are Official — Welcome Home, Partner
completionBody: Space Cowboys, Inc. has a permanent kiosk in the Mars spaceport. Your name is on the door. I wrote it in marker — should outlast the hull plating, definitely outlasts the warranty.
From here on, Mars is home base. Open your star map and click the planet anytime you want to fast-travel back. The orbital traffic controllers will pretend they slotted you in legitimately.
Drinks at the kiosk. On me. I have been waiting eleven years for an excuse to say that.
